c74object.name: dk.corpusconvolver~
c74object.description: The dk.corpusconvolver~ object works in conjunction with dk.descriptors~ or dk.descriptorsframe to find the nearest match in a pre-analyzed corpus similar to how dk.corpusmatch works. However instead of simply returning the name of the file that has matched, it loads the sample as an IR that the incoming audio is convolved with.
c74object.discussion: Based on multiconvolve~ from the HIRT package. For more info visit https://cycling74.com/packages/hisstools-impulse-response-toolbox-hirt

inlet.description: Primary input for onset detection and recording. Different input modes can apply filtering based on the type of input being used.
Audio input for recording independently of onset detection when being used in different input modes.

outlet.description: Analyzed onset as a trigger, time-aligned with descriptor analysis.
Analyzed onset as a gate, time-aligned with descriptor analysis.

objarg.name: default
objarg.digest: JSON file to load by default
objarg.description: JSON file to load by default.

objarg.name: setup
objarg.digest: Setup JSON file to load by default
objarg.description: Setup JSON file to load by default.

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dumps out the current entries as a list
method.description: Dumps out current entries as a list (filtered or otherwise) as a list.

method.name: dumpdict
method.digest: Dumps out the current entries as a list
method.description: Dumps out current entries as a list (filtered or otherwise) as a dictionary.

method.name: info
method.digest: Print corpus metatdata/info to Max console
method.description: Prints a summary of the corpus metatdata/info to the Max console.

method.name: print
method.digest: Print dataset contents to Max console
method.description: Prints a summary of the descriptors in the dataset to the Max console.

method.name: read
method.description: Reads a pre-trained corpus .json file directly from a relative or absolute path. With no additional argument, a file dialog will appear to choose a .json file.

method.name: refer
method.description:
Messages related to buffer~ loading and referring.
You can point the analysis to an existing buffer~ by sending sending a refer message followed by a valid buffer~ name. This will immediately analyze the named buffer~ and make it available for playback as soon as the process is done.
Additional optional arguments allow you to specify a subsection of the buffer~ to load for analysis.
A single additional argument will specify how much of the buffer~ (in ms) to analyze from the start. If two arguments are given these will specify the start position (in ms) of the analysis and then the duration (in ms) of the buffer~ to analyze after that point.
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
refer bufferName or refer bufferName 500 or refer bufferName 500 1500

attribute.name: active
attribute.digest: Enables or disables the matching
attribute.description: Enables or disables the matching.

attribute.name: corpus
attribute.digest: Messages related to corpus matching
attribute.description:
Messages related to corpus matching.
After sending the corpus message you send an argument of 'match' followed by a time-scale you wish to match against.
The available options are:
short = the first 256 samples (at 44.1k sample rate)
medium = the first 4410 samples (at 44.1k sample rate)
all = the entire file
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
corpus match all or corpus match small

attribute.name: envelope
attribute.digest: Determines whether differential thresholding is logarithmic or linear
attribute.description: Determines whether ampltidue thresholding is computed on the logarithmic differential (default) or linear (log or lin).

attribute.name: fastrampdown
attribute.digest: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling
attribute.description: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling. Typically, this will be faster than slowrampdown.

attribute.name: fastrampup
attribute.digest: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising
attribute.description: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising. Typically, this will be faster than slowrampup.

attribute.name: fftsettings
attribute.digest: FFT settings for analysis
attribute.description:
FFT settings consist of three numbers representing the window size, hop size and FFT size in samples:
The hop size and fft size can both be set to -1 (and are by default), with slightly different meanings:
For the hop size, -1 = windowsize/2
For the FFT size, -1 = windowsize snapped to the nearest equal / greater power of 2 (e.g. windowsize 1024 => fftsize 1024, but windowssize 1000 also => fftsize 1024)
Constraints
FFTSize, if != -1, will snap to the next greatest power of two
if FFT size != -1, then window size is clipped at FFT size

attribute.name: filter
attribute.digest: Messages related to corpus filtering
attribute.description:
Messages related to corpus filtering. After sending the filter message you send a series of additional arguments that determine what descriptor is being filtered, how it is being filtered, as well as further filtering strands combined with conditionals.
The available descriptors that can be used for filtering are:
duration = overall sample duration in ms
time_centroid = how long a sample 'sounds' in ms
loudness = loudness in dB
centroid = centroid in MIDI pitch
flatness = spectral flatness in dB
pitch = pitch in MIDI pitch
pitch_confidence = pitch confidence in 0 to 1
loudness_medium = same for medium time frame
centroid_medium = same for medium time frame
flatness_medium = same for medium time frame
pitch_medium = same for medium time frame
pitch_confidence_medium = same for medium time frame
loudness_all = same for entire sample
centroid_all = same for entire sample
flatness_all = same for entire sample
pitch_all = same for entire sample
pitch_confidence_all = same for entire sample
index = the sample index in the corpus
You can filter by the following operators:
== = equals
!= = does not equal
< = less than
<= = less than or equal
> = greater than
>= = greater than or equal
You can join multiple queries using:
and
or
Therefore a complete message would look as follows: filter loudness < -45 or filter duration > 2500 or filter centroid > 60 and time_centroid < 80
To reset the filtering you can send the following message: 'filter reset'

attribute.name: floor
attribute.digest: The level in dB the slow ramp needs to be above to consider a detected difference valid, allowing to ignore the onsets in the noise floor
attribute.description: The level in dB the slow ramp needs to be above to consider a detected difference valid, allowing to ignore the onsets in the noise floor.

attribute.name: gain
attribute.digest: Output gain
attribute.description: Output gain (in %).

attribute.name: highpass
attribute.digest: Highpass frequency cutoff for impulse
attribute.description: Highpass frequency cutoff for impulse.

attribute.name: highpassfreq
attribute.digest: The frequency of the fourth-order high-pass filter. This is done first on the signal to minimise low frequency intermodulation with very fast ramp lengths.
attribute.description: The frequency of the fourth-order Linkwitz–Riley high-pass filter (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linkwitz%E2%80%93Riley_filter). This is done first on the signal to minimise low frequency intermodulation with very fast ramp lengths. A frequency of 0 bypasses the filter.

attribute.name: highshelf
attribute.digest: Boost/cut for highshelf filter set at 1.5k
attribute.description: Boost/cut for highshelf filter set at 1.5k (in dB).

attribute.name: impulse
attribute.digest: Amount of impulse signal sent to convolution
attribute.description: Amount of impulse signal sent to convolution (in %).

attribute.name: input
attribute.digest: Sets the input mode (air microphone, SP Sensor, contact/trigger)
attribute.description:
Sets the input mode allowing you to use an air microphone, a Sensory Percussion sensor, or a normal drum trigger/contact mic. When using input modes 3/4, the SP sensor and contact mic are only used for onset detection, and the air microphone will be recorded into the buffer~ for audio analysis.
The available options are:
0 = primary input (onset and recording)
1 = SP sensor in primary input (onset and recording)
2 = contact mic in primary input (onset and recording)
3 = SP sensor + air microphone (secondary input)
4 = contact mic + air microphone (secondary input)
5 = primary input + secondary input (recording)
6 = SP sensor with mic correction

attribute.name: invertmatching
attribute.digest: Returns matches that are furthest away
attribute.description: Returns matches that are furthest away as opposed to the nearest matches. This is useful for creating contrast between the analyzed audio and the loaded corpus.

attribute.name: lockout
attribute.digest: Amount of time (in ms) before it can get triggered again
attribute.description: Amount of time (in ms) before an onset can get triggered again.

attribute.name: metric
attribute.digest: The metric on which spectral difference is computed
attribute.description:
The metric used to derive a difference curve between spectral frames. It can be any of the following:
0 = '0'
Energy thresholds on (sum of squares of magnitudes / nBins) (like Onsets power)
1 = '1'
HFC thresholds on (sum of (squared magnitudes * binNum) / nBins)
2 = '2'
SpectralFlux thresholds on (difference in magnitude between consecutive frames, half rectified)
3 = '3'
MKL thresholds on (sum of log of magnitude ratio per bin) (or equivalently, sum of difference of the log magnitude per bin) (like Onsets mkl)
4 = '4'
IS Itakura-Saito divergence (see literature)
5 = '5'
Cosine thresholds on (cosine distance between comparison frames)
6 = '6'
PhaseDev takes the past 2 frames, projects to the current, as anticipated if it was a steady state, then compute the sum of the differences, on which it thresholds (like Onsets phase)
7 = '7'
WPhaseDev same as PhaseDev, but weighted by the magnitude in order to remove chaos noise floor (like Onsets wphase)
8 = '8'
ComplexDev same as PhaseDev, but in the complex domain - the anticipated amp is considered steady, and the phase is projected, then a complex subtraction is done with the actual present frame. The sum of magnitudes is used to threshold (like Onsets complex)
9 = '9'
RComplexDev same as above, but rectified (like Onsets rcomplex)

attribute.name: minslicelength
attribute.digest: The length in samples that the slice will stay ON
attribute.description: The length in samples that the Slice will stay ON. Changes of states during that period will be ignored. This is the equivalent of lockout but set in samples.

attribute.name: mode
attribute.digest: Select between amplitude or spectral onset detection algorithms
attribute.description: Select between amplitude or spectral onset detection algorithms.

attribute.name: noise
attribute.digest: Amount of noise added to the impulse signal
attribute.description: Amount of noise added to the impulse signal.

attribute.name: offthreshold
attribute.digest: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to reset, aka to allow the differential envelope to trigger again.
attribute.description: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to reset, aka to allow the differential envelope to trigger again.

attribute.name: onthreshold
attribute.digest: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to trigger an onset, aka to go ON when in OFF state
attribute.description: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to trigger an onset, aka to go ON when in OFF state. It is computed on the difference between the two envelope followers.

attribute.name: pickup
attribute.digest: Pickup parameter in impulse generator algorithm
attribute.description: Pickup parameter in impulse generator algorithm.

attribute.name: position
attribute.digest: Position parameter in impulse generator algorithm
attribute.description: Position parameter in impulse generator algorithm.

attribute.name: predictedweight
attribute.digest: Scaling of predicted descriptors
attribute.description: Relative scaling of predicted descriptors when using predicted time scale (0. to 1.).

attribute.name: roundrobin
attribute.digest: Enable roundrobin sample matching
attribute.description:
Enables round robin sample matching so when a match is found twice in a row, a randomized selection is made within the nearest matches.
The available options are:
0 = round robin selection is off
1 = round robin selection is on (default 5 nearest neighbors)

attribute.name: select
attribute.digest: Select a subset of descriptors to pass through
attribute.description: Allow only a subset of the descriptors to come out of the list output (buffer~ output is unaffected).

attribute.name: default
attribute.value: loudness, loudness_derivative, centroid, centroid_derivative, flatness, flatness_derivative, pitch, pitch_confidence

attribute.name: sensitivity
attribute.digest: The nervousness of the algorithm thresholds (from 0 to 100%)
attribute.description: The nervousness of the algorithm thresholds (from 0 to 100%). This is essentially a 'macro' parameter for the individual on/off thresholds.

attribute.name: setup
attribute.digest: Messages related to setups
attribute.description:
Messages related to setups.
After sending the setup message you can send send several additional arguments. These are:
load = opens file dialog to select pre-analyzed setup .json file.
read = reads a pre-analyzed setup .json file from a relative or absolute path
match = enables or disables the setup matching by sending a 0 or 1
range = sets the median and range for the internal standardization. this consists of two values between 0-100 with the default being 25 75
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
setup range 10 90 or setup match 1 or setup read setup_snare.json

attribute.name: slowrampdown
attribute.digest: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling.
attribute.description: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling.

attribute.name: slowrampup
attribute.digest: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising.
attribute.description: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising.

attribute.name: smoothing
attribute.digest: The size of the smoothing filter applied to the difference curve
attribute.description: The size of the smoothing filter applied to the difference curve when in spectral mode.

attribute.name: weights
attribute.digest: Messages related to descriptor weighting
attribute.description:
Messages related to descriptor weighting.
After sending the weights message you send a series of additional arguments that determine what descriptor is being weighted, and how much its weight is being modified by.
The available descriptors that can be used for weighting are:
loudness
loudness_derivative
centroid
centroid_derivative
flatness
flatness_derivative
pitch
pitch_confidence
The descriptors are weighted with a percentage between 0% and 1000%, with 100% being the default.
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
weights loudness 200 or weights pitch 1000 or weights flatness 50, weights pitch_confidence 500

attribute.name: window
attribute.digest: Window of time to look for spectral difference in (for HFC, SpectralFlux, MKL, Cosine only)
attribute.description: Window of time to look for spectral difference in when in spectral mode (for HFC, SpectralFlux, MKL, Cosine only). (in ms)
